mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO increased, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.0% signals excess labor slack that depresses wage growth and household consumption forecasts, leading equity investors to downgrade earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and a low nominal federal funds rate produce deeply negative real policy rates, which forces equity investors to mark down long-duration cash flows abruptly, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels shows no incremental explanatory power over additive single-series models (F-test p > 0.05), or SPX returns are positive in more than 45% of joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is already tight, a deceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth indicates that employment momentum is exhausted and recession risk is rising, causing equity risk premia to expand and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly lower during PAYEMS deceleration episodes when UNRATE is below 4.5% compared to when UNRATE is above 4.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal is profitable less than 52% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in retail sales indicates resilient consumer spending that supports near-term revenue beats, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases do not coincide with positive SPX 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate reduces the cost of corporate borrowing and raises the present value of future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Fed funds rate decreases are followed by negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the walk-forward information coefficient is negative over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP signals persistent aggregate demand expansion that validates higher earnings growth trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth surprises are associated with negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 45% of quarterly observations, or the strategy Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while unemployment is simultaneously rising, stagflationary pressure crushes profit margins and forces multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases over prior month and CPIAUCSL increases over prior month
failure_condition: Joint increases in UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative SPX 5-day returns in more than 60% of coincident observations, or the interaction term does not significantly improve R-squared relative to main effects (F-test p > 0.05).
